purpose: Develop a pragmatic worker safety model for sandboxed, artifact-scoped execution: keep only non-negotiable invariants hard, reduce unnecessary friction, and provide explicit profiles (safe, balanced, fast) that preserve hub ownership, traceability and reliability. Execution is fully automated with no interactive user-in-the-loop steps and uses incremental retrieval plus stepwise planning instead of full-repository prompt dumps.
goal: Make the ananta worker noticeably more productive in sandbox mode while preserving core guarantees: schema-valid artifacts, bounded execution, policy visibility and audit-grade traceability.
background_assumptions: Worker runs in a sandbox/worktree and does not own orchestration decisions.
Hub prepares bounded context/artifacts and remains control-plane owner.
Worker returns artifacts/results back to hub; mutation paths remain policy-governed.
Primary payload model is files and diffs; small manifests carry only control metadata and contracts.
Execution is fully automated: no manual user prompts, confirmations or runtime interventions.
Sandbox isolation alone is insufficient against prompt-injection-style instruction poisoning and hidden quality drift.
mandatory_invariants: Strict schema validation for inbound and outbound artifacts.
Capability and command allowlist enforcement by risk class.
Hard budgets for iterations, runtime, file count and byte volume.
Complete traceability fields (trace_id, task_id, capability_id, context_hash, policy_decision_ref).
relaxable_controls: Lower automated policy-gate friction for low-risk commands inside sandbox profile.
More aggressive auto-repair loop in fast profile with bounded retries.
Reduced default-deny surface for read-only and deterministic diagnostic commands.
Adaptive context/file selection limits instead of one rigid global limit.
non_goals: Do not remove hub-owned policy/approval/audit ownership.
Do not permit unbounded repository execution context.
Do not silently treat degraded or denied outcomes as success.
Do not make optional external adapters required for baseline worker path.
Do not split core behavior into incompatible hub and standalone implementations.
success_criteria: Balanced profile becomes the default and measurably reduces blocked-but-safe executions.
Safe profile retains current strict behavior for sensitive environments.
Fast profile remains bounded and auditable, never bypassing hard invariants.
Native worker path is integrated end-to-end for propose/execute/verify flow where configured.
Worker profile selection and policy decisions are visible in task history and read models.
Prompt-injection-like artifact inputs are explicitly degraded/blocked with machine-readable reasons.
Worker uses incremental retrieval/indexing so only relevant context chunks are loaded per step, not the full repository by default.
Worker maintains a task-local plan artifact (plan.md or equivalent structured form) and executes steps iteratively with bounded budgets.
Worker can run as a standalone package/CLI with file and diff artifacts plus a minimal control manifest, without requiring the full ananta stack.
No execution path requires interactive user action; all gating is machine-decided by policy and contracts.
Regression and e2e tests cover safe, balanced and fast profiles.

milestones.title: Native runtime integration and control surfaces

tasks.title: Worker strictness decision record add
acceptance_criteria: ADR defines hard invariants vs relaxable controls for sandboxed worker execution.
ADR defines profile intent: safe, balanced, fast.
ADR confirms hub remains policy and automated approval owner.
ADR includes migration strategy from current strict defaults.

tasks.title: Mandatory invariant checklist for worker paths add
acceptance_criteria: Checklist covers schema gate, allowlist, budgets and trace metadata requirements.
Checklist is referenced by worker docs and release flow.
Existing trace tests confirm required metadata remains enforced for execution-capable modes.

tasks.title: Worker profile schema and contract add
acceptance_criteria: Schema defines safe/balanced/fast profile fields for policy and budgets.
Schema supports explicit automated policy-gate behavior by risk class without interactive prompts.
Contract tests validate profile examples.

tasks.title: Runtime default profile config add
acceptance_criteria: Balanced profile is default for local developer flow.
Safe profile can be selected explicitly for stricter governance.
Fast profile is opt-in and clearly marked as higher risk.

tasks.title: Profile-aware command policy classification implement
acceptance_criteria: Policy classification uses execution profile and command risk class.
Low-risk read/diagnostic commands can be auto-allow in balanced profile.
Destructive or ambiguous commands remain denied or require hub-issued approval tokens across all profiles, without user interaction.
Tests cover profile deltas and unchanged deny behavior.

tasks.title: Profile-aware patch apply gating semantics implement
acceptance_criteria: Patch apply remains policy-bound when mutating guarded roots.
Profile controls only bounded low-risk sandbox semantics without bypassing hub-issued automated approval bindings.
Automated approval binding checks (task_id/capability_id/context_hash/hash) remain mandatory.

tasks.title: Controlled loop budgets become profile-driven
acceptance_criteria: Safe/balanced/fast map to distinct max_iterations, patch_attempts and runtime budgets.
Budget exhaustion remains explicit stop_reason.
Fast profile increases throughput but never unbounds runtime.

tasks.title: Profile-aware file and context limits implement
acceptance_criteria: Profile controls file count and byte budget in selection stage.
Oversize input remains degraded with explicit reason.
Prompt assembly keeps redaction and max-context boundaries.

tasks.title: Native worker execution path integrate into task-scoped runtime
acceptance_criteria: ananta-worker path executes native worker stages for propose/execute/verify when profile enabled.
SGPT proxy fallback remains available but no longer the only ananta-worker behavior.
Routing metadata clearly indicates native vs fallback path.

tasks.title: Worker profile propagation in routing and execution context add
acceptance_criteria: Execution context includes selected worker profile and profile source.
Task history and routing record preserve profile for observability.
Unknown profile values are rejected or normalized explicitly.

tasks.title: Artifact schema validation gate on worker ingress/egress add
acceptance_criteria: Inbound artifacts are validated before execution attempts.
Outbound artifacts are validated before returning to hub.
Validation failures return explicit degraded state and machine reason.

tasks.title: Prompt-injection hardening for artifact-sourced context add
acceptance_criteria: Artifact instructions conflicting with hub policy are ignored or degraded.
Dangerous command synthesis patterns from context are blocked before execution planning.
E2E test demonstrates explicit blocked/degraded response for injected hostile instructions.

tasks.title: Worker degraded reason taxonomy refine
acceptance_criteria: Reason taxonomy distinguishes policy denied, hub approval token missing, schema invalid, budget exhausted and unsafe command.
Reason names are stable and machine-readable.
Docs map reasons to operator actions.

tasks.title: Profile and policy observability fields extend
acceptance_criteria: Trace/routing payload includes worker_profile, profile_source and policy_classification summary.
Read-model fields support operator explanation of profile-driven behavior.
No backward-incompatible removal of existing routing fields.

tasks.title: Metrics for profile effectiveness and failure modes add
acceptance_criteria: Metrics include block-rate, degrade-rate, approval-rate and success-rate by worker profile.
Metrics distinguish policy-denied from runtime failure.
SLO docs mention profile-level interpretation.

tasks.title: Profile regression test matrix add
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover safe, balanced and fast profile behavior for commands, loops and gating.
Critical deny cases are invariant across profiles.
Balanced profile demonstrates lower friction for explicitly low-risk sandbox commands.

tasks.title: Rollout guardrails and feature flags add
acceptance_criteria: Native balanced worker path is rollout-gated by config flag/profile switch.
Fallback path remains selectable for recovery.
No rollout mode introduces interactive user approval dependencies at runtime.
Rollback procedure is documented and tested at config level.

tasks.title: Worker profile operations runbook update
acceptance_criteria: Runbook explains when to use safe, balanced and fast profiles.
Docs include troubleshooting for blocked/degraded outcomes by profile.
Docs explain that sandbox != no policy and why hard invariants remain mandatory.
Docs state that the worker execution flow is fully automated and user intervention is not part of runtime control.

tasks.title: Embedding provider abstraction and index contract add
acceptance_criteria: Index contract defines chunk metadata, embedding version, source path and content hash fields.
Embedding provider is abstraction-based so local/open-source and hosted providers can be swapped without core loop changes.
Contract validation rejects stale or malformed index entries explicitly.

tasks.title: Incremental repository indexing strategy implement
acceptance_criteria: Indexing supports bootstrap plus incremental refresh by changed files/content hash.
Chunking strategy is deterministic and language-aware where available.
Repository-wide full-context send is disabled by default in favor of indexed retrieval.

tasks.title: Budgeted retrieval and reranking pipeline integrate
acceptance_criteria: Retrieval pipeline ranks candidates by task relevance and enforces profile-specific file/byte/token budgets.
Context expansion is iterative: retrieve, attempt step, verify, then retrieve more only when needed.
Trace includes retrieval decisions (selected paths/chunks and exclusion reasons) for auditability.

tasks.title: Task-local plan artifact and stepwise execution loop add
acceptance_criteria: Worker creates and updates a task-local plan artifact (plan.md or machine-readable equivalent) during execution.
Execution progresses step-by-step against the current plan with explicit step state transitions.
Plan updates are automatic and non-interactive; no user confirmation is required during runtime.

tasks.title: Standalone mode boundary contract define
acceptance_criteria: Boundary between worker core and hub integration is explicitly defined via ports/contracts.
Standalone contract supports file and diff payloads with minimal control metadata.
Core runtime path does not require hub database/session internals.

tasks.title: Hub integration adapters refactor behind worker ports
acceptance_criteria: Worker core loop depends on abstractions, not direct hub service imports.
Existing hub-integrated runtime remains supported via adapters.
Standalone runtime executes the same core loop with injected policy/trace interfaces.

tasks.title: Standalone CLI and container runtime add
acceptance_criteria: CLI executes fully non-interactive against a workspace plus control manifest.
CLI supports safe/balanced/fast profiles and retrieval/index configuration.
Run output includes machine-readable result and artifact manifest (diffs, traces, degraded reasons).

tasks.title: Standalone compatibility and e2e flow tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover both greenfield project bootstrap and existing-project continuation flows.
Standalone mode emits the same critical trace/degradation semantics as hub mode.
Docs list constraints, migration path and compatibility expectations between standalone and hub execution.

execution_order_summary: Define profile contract and hard invariants first.
Introduce embedding/index contracts and incremental indexing before broad retrieval usage.
Implement profile-aware command/patch/budget controls before runtime wiring.
Integrate budgeted retrieval and reranking to keep context minimal and relevant per step.
Integrate native worker path into task-scoped execution with explicit routing metadata.
Define standalone boundary contracts and extract hub-specific integrations behind adapters.
Harden schema validation and artifact-sourced prompt handling against injection patterns.
Add task-local plan artifact support for iterative planning and stepwise automated execution.
Provide standalone CLI/container entrypoints that run the same core loop non-interactively.
Add full profile matrix tests before enabling rollout by default.
Ship docs and operational guidance together with configuration flags.
summary_notes: The worker can be significantly less strict in sandbox mode, but only after hard invariants are isolated and preserved.
Balanced should become the practical default for developer productivity, with safe and fast as explicit operational choices.
The target operating model is explicitly no-user-in-loop: decisions are policy-driven and machine-enforced.
Retrieval should be incremental and index-driven (embedding-backed), so the LLM receives only relevant context instead of full repository payloads.
Planning should be explicit and stepwise via a task-local plan artifact that is updated automatically during execution.
The worker should be consumable standalone via stable contracts and adapters, while preserving one shared core execution loop.
This track intentionally focuses on policy-calibration and native path integration, not broad architecture refactors.
